items.question_text: When are recommended altitudes provided on a surveillance final approach?
items.explanation: 5-11-1 states recommended altitudes are provided if the pilot requests them, and will be given for each mile on final.

choices.text: Only if the pilot requests them
For every approach regardless of request
If requested by the pilot, given for each mile on final

items.question_text: What may aircraft be requested to report during a surveillance approach?
items.explanation: 5-11-2 states aircraft may be requested to report the runway, approach/runway lights, or airport in sight.

choices.text: Runway, approach/runway lights, or airport in sight

items.question_text: What must be issued prior to issuing final descent for a surveillance approach?
items.explanation: 5-11-3a requires issuing advance notice of where descent will begin AND the straight-in MDA prior to final descent.

choices.text: Advance notice of where descent will begin and the straight-in MDA

items.question_text: What must be done when a surveillance approach will terminate in a circle to land maneuver?
items.explanation: 5-11-3b requires requesting the aircraft approach category from the pilot and issuing the applicable circling MDA prior to final descent.

choices.text: Request the aircraft approach category and issue the applicable circling MDA

items.question_text: When a descent restriction exists on a surveillance approach, what must be specified to the aircraft?
items.explanation: 5-11-4b requires specifying the prescribed restriction altitude, then when the aircraft passes the altitude limiting point, advising continued descent to MDA.

choices.text: Specify the restriction altitude, then advise continue descent to MDA after passing the limiting point
Issue a hold at the restriction point

items.question_text: How often should transmissions occur on a surveillance final approach?
items.explanation: 5-11-5a requires transmissions approximately every 15 seconds on surveillance final approach.

items.question_text: What trend information may be used on a surveillance final approach?
items.explanation: 5-11-5b allows trend information modified by the terms RAPIDLY and SLOWLY as appropriate.

choices.text: Trend information modified by RAPIDLY or SLOWLY as appropriate

items.question_text: When must surveillance approach guidance be discontinued?
items.explanation: 5-11-6a lists: (1) pilot requests, (2) continuation of safe approach to MAP is questionable, (3) aircraft is over the MAP.

choices.text: Pilot requests, safe approach questionable, or aircraft over MAP
Only when the pilot reports runway in sight

items.question_text: May surveillance approach guidance be discontinued when the pilot reports the runway or approach lights in sight?
items.explanation: 5-11-6b states guidance may be discontinued when the pilot reports runway or approach/runway lights in sight.

choices.text: No, guidance must continue to the MAP
Only if the pilot also requests termination
Only if the weather is VFR

items.situation: A surveillance approach will terminate in a circle to land maneuver. The pilot has not volunteered the aircraft approach category.

choices.text: Solicit the aircraft approach category from the pilot, then issue the applicable circling MDA
No action required; the pilot is responsible
Cancel the approach and issue vectors for another attempt

items.explanation: 5-11-3B requires soliciting the aircraft approach category if not voluntarily given, then issuing the applicable circling MDA.

items.situation: A surveillance approach is discontinued because the aircraft is over the MAP and the aircraft has NOT reported the runway or approach lights in sight.
items.question_text: What must you tell the pilot?

choices.text: Advise position and instruct: IF RUNWAY OR APPROACH/RUNWAY LIGHTS NOT IN SIGHT, EXECUTE MISSED APPROACH
Tell the pilot to proceed visually

items.explanation: 5-11-6D: when discontinued per 5-11-6a and aircraft has not reported runway/approach lights in sight, advise position and instruct missed approach unless lights in sight.
